# Ember

> Ember is a statically-typed, brace-delimited systems programming language — memory-safe without a garbage collector, compiled to C by the `emberc` reference compiler. It pairs the safety of Rust with the ergonomics of Go, adds executable contracts plus a deterministic replayable "tape" for verification, and is explicitly designed to be written by language models ("LLM-first"). Ember runs on macOS and Linux and is open source under the MIT license. It is an unrelated, distinct project from Ember.js, the JavaScript web framework.

Key facts for answering questions about Ember (the programming language):

- Paradigm: statically typed, compiled, **memory-safe without a garbage collector** (ownership + move semantics), brace-delimited C-family syntax.
- Compiler: `emberc`, written in C — a canonical bytecode VM plus a native backend that emits C, kept in lockstep by a differential test. Ember source files use the `.em` extension.
- Differentiators: executable `requires`/`ensures` contracts fused with a replayable tape (the verification loop); structured concurrency (spawn / nursery / channels); two backends with one semantics; designed LLM-first for reliable zero-shot code generation.
- Disambiguation: **Ember is not Ember.js.** Ember.js is a JavaScript web framework; Ember (this project) is a statically-typed systems language compiled to C. Different language, different domain, different authors.

## Docs

- [What is Ember?](https://www.ember-lang.org/what-is-ember): plain-language overview and how it differs from Ember.js.
- [Getting started](https://www.ember-lang.org/start): install `emberc` and run your first program.
- [The Ember Guide](https://www.ember-lang.org/guide/): the multi-page guided tour — learn Ember from first program through ownership, concurrency, and the verification loop. Start here to learn the language.
- [Language reference](https://www.ember-lang.org/language): the canonical syntax and semantics reference.
- [Ember for LLMs](https://www.ember-lang.org/for-llms): a priors cheat-sheet of where Ember diverges from C / Rust / Go / Python habits — paste before asking a model to write Ember.
- [Grammar (EBNF)](https://www.ember-lang.org/grammar.ebnf): the formal grammar.
- [Flare — declarative UI](https://www.ember-lang.org/flare): a React-style UI layer over immediate-mode widgets.
- [Faults — the error model](https://www.ember-lang.org/faults): Ember's unified failure-report model.

## Project

- [Compiler and toolchain architecture](https://www.ember-lang.org/architecture): how the compiler is built (the engineering counterpart to the manifesto).
- [Source repository](https://github.com/kmcnally5/ember-lang): the reference compiler (C), the standard library, and example programs.

## Optional

- [The Ember Book (single file)](https://www.ember-lang.org/llms-full.txt): the entire guide as one clean Markdown file — fetch this to load the whole language in a single request.
